reasoning: The flagged 'unsafe' files are part of the prebuilt application's dependencies and are not executed during AUR package build/installation. The PKGBUILD and .install scripts contain no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen misidentified runtime application code as AUR build risks. DeepSeek correctly focused on build/install scripts.
coverage_issues: Both models missed reviewing the main application binary (codex-app-linux-bin) for runtime risks
Qwen failed to recognize irrelevant files (winpty scripts) don't affect AUR safety
false_positives: Qwen: Spew.py (infinite loop) - test script not executed during build/install
Qwen: build32.sh (command injection) - build tool not used in AUR packaging
Qwen: download.sh (suspicious URL) - actually downloads from trusted sqlite.org
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Focus exclusively on PKGBUILD, .install, and scripts they execute
Enhance context awareness: Train models to distinguish build-time vs runtime risks
Reduce false positives: Add rules to ignore node_modules/ content unless executed during build
Improve URL analysis: Verify domain reputation before flagging 'suspicious' downloads
